title: Hades
blurb: Greek mythology roguelike. Tight runs, real story, gets out of your way.

title: A Short Hike
blurb: Climb a mountain in an afternoon. Nothing wants anything from you.

title: Stardew Valley
blurb: Inherit a farm, slowly rebuild a life. Easy to load, hard to put down.

title: Outer Wilds
blurb: Knowledge-based exploration. You'll know when to stop, and that's the point.

title: Hollow Knight
blurb: Beautiful, brutal, enormous. The pile fears this one.

title: Celeste
blurb: Hard platformer, surprisingly tender. Beatable in a weekend.

title: Disco Elysium
blurb: RPG that's mostly conversation. You play a wreck of a detective. Read it like a novel.

title: Vampire Survivors
blurb: 20-minute runs, dopamine on tap. Costs less than a coffee.

title: Slay the Spire
blurb: Deckbuilder roguelike. Each run teaches the next. Endlessly replayable.

title: Inscryption
blurb: Card game in a cabin. Goes weird places. Trust it.

title: Slime Rancher 2
blurb: Slimes, plorts, no stakes. Background-music for your brain.

title: Tunic
blurb: Tiny fox, huge world. The manual is part of the puzzle.

title: Cult of the Lamb
blurb: Roguelike + cult management. Cuter and meaner than it looks.

title: Chained Echoes
blurb: Modern JRPG that respects your time. SNES energy, 2020s polish.

title: Pizza Tower
blurb: Cartoon platformer at 200mph. Pure adrenaline, beatable in a week.

title: Subnautica
blurb: Ocean survival. Beautiful and terrifying in equal measure.

title: Return of the Obra Dinn
blurb: Detective puzzle on a dead ship. One weekend, no regrets.

title: Tetris Effect
blurb: Tetris if Tetris was a religious experience. Headphones non-negotiable.

title: Pentiment
blurb: 16th-century murder mystery, illuminated-manuscript art style. Slow burn, deeply rewarding.

title: Balatro
blurb: Poker roguelike that ate everyone's December. One more run. One more.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
blurb: The one that taught a generation what adventure feels like. Still holds up.

title: Soulcalibur
blurb: The fighting game that made Dreamcast owners insufferable. Deservedly.

title: Super Mario Galaxy
blurb: Nintendo threw Mario into space and somehow made gravity fun. Pure joy.

title: Metroid Prime
blurb: Lonely, atmospheric, brilliant. Scanning everything isn't a chore here.

title: Super Mario Galaxy 2
blurb: More Galaxy. That's the highest compliment.

title: Perfect Dark
blurb: GoldenEye's cooler older sister. Laptop guns that become turrets.

title: Super Mario Odyssey
blurb: Possessing a T-Rex with a hat is peak game design. No notes.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
blurb: They broke the Zelda formula and rebuilt it better. Go anywhere.

title: Baldur's Gate III
blurb: 200 hours of 'one more conversation.' Your save file has consequences.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
blurb: Breath of the Wild but now you can glue rockets to a raft.

title: Half-Life
blurb: Started the 'shooters can have stories' argument. Won it immediately.

title: Uncharted 2: Among Thieves
blurb: The train level. You know the one. Blockbuster gaming at its peak.

title: Red Dead Redemption 2
blurb: Slow, deliberate, devastating. Arthur Morgan deserves your time.

title: BioShock
blurb: Would you kindly clear your evening? Rapture doesn't let go.

title: Half-Life 2
blurb: The gravity gun changed everything. Still the benchmark for physics gameplay.

title: Tekken 3
blurb: The fighting game your cousin was suspiciously good at. Still tight.

title: Metal Gear Solid 2: Sons of Liberty
blurb: Kojima predicted the internet and nobody believed him. They do now.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask
blurb: Three days to save the world. Dark, urgent, unforgettable.

title: Baldur's Gate II: Shadows of Amn
blurb: The CRPG everything else is measured against. Minsc and Boo demand it.

title: Portal 2
blurb: Co-op ruins friendships. Solo ruins your sense of what puzzles can be. Perfect.

title: LittleBigPlanet
blurb: Sackboy is pure vibes. Play the levels, skip the creator unless you want to lose a weekend.

title: The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past
blurb: Top-down Zelda perfected. Every dungeon earns its place.

title: Grand Theft Auto IV
blurb: Bowling with your cousin. Also: the best story Rockstar ever told.

title: The Last Of Us
blurb: You'll feel things you didn't sign up for. Trust the pacing.

title: Red Dead Redemption
blurb: The sunset ride home. If you know, you know. If you don't, go find out.

title: XCOM 2: War of the Chosen
blurb: Your soldiers will die and you'll name the next squad after them.

title: Elden Ring
blurb: Beautiful, brutal, enormous. The pile fears this one.

title: The Orange Box
blurb: Three legendary games for the price of one. Valve never did this again.

title: Burnout 3: Takedown
blurb: Pure destruction at 200mph. No simulation, no pretense. Just carnage.

title: Chrono Cross
blurb: Gorgeous, strange, polarizing. The soundtrack alone justifies the hours.

title: Resident Evil Code: Veronica
blurb: Classic RE at its most unhinged. Claire deserves this spotlight.

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion
blurb: The NPCs have unhinged conversations at 3am. You'll love every second.

title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City
blurb: Neon, synths, Hawaiian shirts. The vibes are immaculate.

title: God of War I
blurb: Angry dad before the reboot made him a sad dad. Pure spectacle.

title: Diablo
blurb: Click demons until they drop loot. Repeat forever. The original loop.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
blurb: You've bought it four times. Might as well finally finish it.

title: Batman: Arkham City
blurb: Be Batman in an open-world prison. The combat still feels incredible.

title: BioShock Infinite
blurb: Columbia is gorgeous. The ending will start arguments. Both are the point.

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition
blurb: Everything is a weapon. Oil plus fire equals tactics. Best co-op RPG ever made.

title: Metal Gear Solid 3: Subsistence
blurb: Snake eats frogs in a jungle. Also: the best story in the series.

title: Divinity: Original Sin - Enhanced Edition
blurb: Larian's first crack at the formula. Rough edges, genuine magic underneath.

title: Metal Gear Solid
blurb: Cardboard box stealth. Fourth-wall demolition. Gaming's first auteur moment.

title: Grim Fandango
blurb: Film noir with skeletons. The writing hasn't aged a day.

title: Command & Conquer
blurb: Tiberium harvesting and FMV cutscenes. RTS before RTS got complicated.

title: Super Mario 64
blurb: Invented 3D platforming and mostly got it right on the first try.

title: Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots
blurb: More movie than game. That's either the pitch or the warning.

title: Mass Effect 2
blurb: Assemble the squad. Do their loyalty missions. Get attached. Survive the finale.

title: Persona 5 Royal
blurb: 100+ hours of style, heart, and time management. The calendar is the real boss.

title: Sid Meier's Civilization IV
blurb: 'One more turn' at 2am. You've been warned.

title: Jet Set Radio
blurb: Cel-shaded rollerblading with a soundtrack that never left your head.

title: God of War (2018)
blurb: One continuous camera shot. Father-son roadtrip through Norse mythology. Heavy.

title: StarCraft II: Wings of Liberty
blurb: The campaign is secretly great. Raynor's story carries it.

title: Company of Heroes
blurb: WW2 RTS where cover and positioning matter more than build order spam.

title: Gears of War 2
blurb: Chainsaw guns and genuine emotional beats. The Horde mode alone is worth it.

title: Dwarf Fortress
blurb: ASCII art hides the deepest simulation ever made. Losing is fun. Literally.

title: Okami
blurb: Zelda by way of Japanese watercolor. Paintbrush combat that never stops being satisfying.

title: Grand Theft Auto III
blurb: The one that started it. Rough now, but the freedom was real.

title: The Last of Us Part II
blurb: Relentless. Will make you feel things you didn't sign up for.

title: Maniac Mansion: Day of the Tentacle
blurb: Point-and-click perfection. Time travel puzzles that actually make sense. Mostly.

title: Castlevania: Symphony of the Night
blurb: The map keeps going. Invented a genre and still plays better than most of its children.

title: SSX 3
blurb: One enormous mountain, top to bottom. Arcade snowboarding that hasn't been topped.

title: Paper Mario (2000)
blurb: RPG with construction-paper charm. Funny, simple, genuinely clever.

title: Skies of Arcadia
blurb: Sky pirates, airship battles, a world that wants to be explored. JRPG comfort food.

title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas
blurb: Three cities, a BMX, and zero chill. The one everyone remembers.

title: Super Mario 3D World
blurb: Pure platforming joy. Every level a new idea, no filler.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword
blurb: Divisive for a reason. Motion controls aside, the dungeons are some of Zelda's best.

title: Homeworld
blurb: Space RTS in full 3D. Still gorgeous. Still heartbreaking.

title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate
blurb: Everyone is here. Literally everyone. Single-player is meatier than you'd expect.

title: God of War II
blurb: Bigger, angrier sequel. The scale goes absurd and earns it.

title: Demon's Souls (2020)
blurb: The remake that justified the PS5. Gorgeous misery from minute one.

title: Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri
blurb: Civilization on an alien planet with better writing than most RPGs.

title: Soul Calibur II
blurb: Weapon-based fighting that still feels incredible. Pick a guest character and go.

title: FINAL FANTASY VI
blurb: The opera scene. Kefka. The World of Ruin. The JRPG all others are measured against.

title: The Walking Dead: Season 1
blurb: Telltale's best. You'll make terrible choices and feel every one.

title: God of War III
blurb: Opens with the most ridiculous set piece in gaming history. Doesn't slow down.

title: Uncharted 3: Drake's Deception
blurb: Big set pieces, great banter. The plane sequence alone.

title: Eternal Darkness: Sanity's Requiem
blurb: Horror that messes with YOU, not just the character. The sanity effects are legendary.

title: Vagrant Story (2000)
blurb: Dark, dense, and criminally underplayed. The weapon crafting will consume you.

title: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe
blurb: The definitive kart racer. 96 tracks. Every single one bangs.

title: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare
blurb: The campaign that rewrote shooters. Short, brutal, unforgettable.

title: Super Mario World
blurb: Perfect 2D platforming. Cape Feather is the best power-up ever made.

title: Metroid Fusion
blurb: Tight, tense, linear Metroid. The SA-X is stalking you. You are not ready.

title: Fire Emblem Awakening
blurb: The one that saved the franchise. Permadeath makes every move matter.

title: Super Mario Sunshine
blurb: Weird Mario. FLUDD is polarizing, but the platforming underneath is pure.

title: Bloodborne
blurb: Victorian nightmare at 30fps and you won't care. The best combat From ever made.

title: Undertale
blurb: Looks like nothing. Plays like nothing else. Spare everyone or don't. It notices.

title: Final Fantasy X
blurb: Linear and proud of it. The story hits harder than the sphere grid.

title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell Chaos Theory
blurb: Peak stealth. Every mission a puzzle box. The bank level is a masterpiece.

title: Final Fantasy XII
blurb: MMO combat in a single-player shell. The Gambit system is programming-lite and brilliant.

title: A Monster's Expedition
blurb: Push trees. Make bridges. The whole island opens up. Quietly perfect.

title: Thief: The Dark Project
blurb: First-person stealth before anyone else figured it out. Sound design carries the game.

title: Warcraft 3: Reign of Chaos
blurb: RTS with hero units and a campaign that set up an entire MMO.

title: Braid
blurb: Time-bending platformer. The last world recontextualizes everything. Short and permanent.

title: Bayonetta 2
blurb: Over-the-top action that never stops escalating. Pure spectacle, zero filler.

title: Metroid Prime 2: Echoes
blurb: Darker, harder Metroid Prime. The light/dark world mechanic earns every headache.

title: Age of Empires II: Age of Kings
blurb: The RTS that ruined a generation's bedtime. Still holds up.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
blurb: Massive, dumb, brilliant. Three playable idiots and a world that rewards curiosity.

title: Conker's Bad Fur Day
blurb: Foul-mouthed platformer from Rare's unhinged era. Absolutely for you.

title: Banjo-Kazooie
blurb: Collect-a-thon perfected. Charming, funny, and tighter than you remember.

title: Journey
blurb: Two hours, no words, genuinely moving. Play it in one sitting.

title: System Shock 2
blurb: The immersive sim that taught BioShock everything. Creaky but still terrifying.

title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
blurb: 200 hours of world-class RPG. Side quests here outwrite most games' main stories.

title: Crash Bandicoot 3: Warped
blurb: Peak Crash. Tight levels, wild variety, beatable in a weekend.

title: Gears of War 3
blurb: Best campaign in the series. A real ending and Horde mode worth it alone.

title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell
blurb: Slow, tense stealth that rewards patience. Sam Fisher's best outing aged well.

title: Crysis
blurb: Still gorgeous. The nanosuit sandbox is more fun than the memes suggest.

title: Shadow of the Colossus
blurb: Sixteen bosses. No filler. Every fight is a puzzle made of fur and stone.

title: The Legend of Zelda: A Link Between Worlds
blurb: Top-down Zelda that trusts you from the start. Open structure, tight dungeons.

title: Klonoa 2: Lunatea's Veil
blurb: Gorgeous 2.5D platformer that nobody played. Short, sweet, surprisingly emotional.

title: Ninja Gaiden (2004)
blurb: Brutal, precise combat that punishes button-mashing. You will die. You will improve.

title: Super Mario World 2: Yoshi's Island
blurb: Hand-drawn platforming at its best. Baby Mario's crying is the final boss.

title: Mark of the Ninja
blurb: Stealth done right in 2D. Every kill feels earned. Tight and replayable.

title: Death Road to Canada
blurb: Roguelike road trip through zombie Canada. Silly, chaotic, great with friends.

title: FEZ
blurb: Rotate the world. 2D becomes 3D becomes puzzle. Quiet, brilliant, unforgettable.

title: Spyro: Year of the Dragon
blurb: Peak PS1 collectathon. More variety than the first two, same charm.

title: Call of Duty
blurb: The original. No killstreaks, no prestige. Just a solid WWII campaign.

title: Resident Evil 2
blurb: Survival horror landmark. Two characters, genuine tension, inventory as gameplay.

title: God of War: Chains of Olympus
blurb: PSP-sized God of War that punches way above its weight.

title: Ratchet & Clank: Up Your Arsenal
blurb: Best PS2 Ratchet. Weapons are creative, humor actually lands.

title: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 2
blurb: Two-minute runs, perfect flow state. The soundtrack alone is worth it.

title: Deus Ex: Human Revolution - Director's Cut
blurb: Cyberpunk immersive sim. Multiple solutions to everything. Your call.

title: Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow
blurb: Best GBA Castlevania. The soul system is addictive. Beatable in a weekend.

title: Dark Souls II
blurb: The weird middle child. Slower, stranger, still rewarding. Majula's music haunts.

title: Baldur's Gate
blurb: The CRPG that started it all. Dense, demanding, worth the learning curve.

title: Halo 3
blurb: The campaign that finished the fight. Still the gold standard for sci-fi shooter setpieces.

title: DRAGON QUEST XI S: Echoes of an Elusive Age - Definitive Edition
blurb: Classic JRPG comfort food. 80 hours of charm, zero cynicism.

title: The Longest Journey
blurb: Point-and-click epic with writing that still holds up. Slow, wordy, rewarding.

title: Rez Infinite
blurb: Synesthesia as a rail shooter. Short, hypnotic, unlike anything else.

title: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
blurb: Best stealth sandbox ever made. The story trails off. The gameplay never does.

title: Fallout 3
blurb: Step out of the vault. The Capital Wasteland is bleak and impossible to stop exploring.

title: Planescape: Torment
blurb: The best-written RPG ever made. Combat is forgettable. Everything else is unforgettable.

title: Dragon Age: Origins
blurb: BioWare's last great RPG. Choices matter, companions are memorable.

title: Ori and the Will of the Wisps
blurb: Gorgeous Metroidvania with combat that actually bites. Better than Blind Forest.

title: Resident Evil
blurb: Fixed cameras, limited ammo, real dread. The original survival horror template still works.

title: Golden Sun
blurb: GBA JRPG with puzzle dungeons and djinn. Comfort-tier handheld RPG.

title: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 4
blurb: Arcade skating at its loosest. Open levels, no timer pressure, just flow.

title: Twisted Metal: Black
blurb: Car combat soaked in grim PS2 atmosphere. Short, violent, singular.

title: Legacy of Kain: Soul Reaver
blurb: Shift between life and death to solve puzzles. The writing carries it hard.

title: Dishonored
blurb: First-person stealth where every room is a puzzle box. Replay it differently every time.

title: Batman: Arkham Asylum
blurb: Tight, contained, the one that proved superhero games could work.

title: Shovel Knight: Treasure Trove
blurb: Four campaigns of retro platforming that earns the nostalgia. Tight and generous.

title: Halo: Reach
blurb: Bungie's farewell. You know how it ends. Play it anyway.

title: N++ (NPLUSPLUS)
blurb: Minimalist platforming, thousands of levels. Zen or rage, your call.

title: Metroid Prime 3: Corruption
blurb: Wii controls finally clicked for a shooter. Weaker story, still great exploration.

title: Gears of War
blurb: Cover shooter that invented the cover shooter. Chainsaw gun. That's the pitch.

title: Grand Theft Auto: Chinatown Wars
blurb: Top-down GTA with actual drug trading mechanics. Handheld, but vicious.

title: Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020
blurb: The entire planet, rendered. Meditative if you let it be.

title: Mario and Luigi: Bowser's Inside Story
blurb: You play as Bowser. The bros are stuck inside him. Funnier than it should be.

title: Tony Hawk's Underground
blurb: Story mode in a skating game. Sounds dumb. It works.

title: ICO
blurb: Hold her hand, solve the castle. Quiet in a way games forgot how to be.

title: Deus Ex
blurb: The immersive sim. Every problem has four solutions. Janky, brilliant, unrepeated.

title: Banjo-Tooie
blurb: Bigger than Kazooie. Maybe too big. Interconnected worlds that reward the stubborn.

title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell: Pandora Tomorrow
blurb: Stealth that punishes impatience. Play it for the shadows.

title: Viewtiful Joe (2003)
blurb: Side-scrolling action with time-bending style. Hard, flashy, short.

title: DRAGON QUEST V
blurb: Three generations of one family. Monster recruiting came first. Comfort food JRPG.

title: Halo: Combat Evolved
blurb: The 30 seconds of fun that built a console. Library level and all.

title: Sea of Stars
blurb: Turn-based RPG with Chrono Trigger energy. Gorgeous, warm, doesn't overstay.

title: Total War: SHOGUN 2
blurb: The tightest Total War. Fewer factions, sharper focus. Campaign eats a week.

title: Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
blurb: Parry or die. No build variety, no crutches. Pure swordplay.

title: Odin Sphere Leifthrasir
blurb: Five characters, one interlocking story. Vanillaware at their most lush.

title: Ape Escape
blurb: Catch monkeys with gadgets. PS1 charm that hasn't been matched since.

title: Donkey Kong 64
blurb: Collect-a-thon that maybe has too much stuff. Nostalgic, exhausting, unforgettable.

title: Mario & Luigi: Superstar Saga
blurb: GBA RPG with timing-based combat. Bros attacks never get old.

title: The Room Three
blurb: Puzzle boxes inside puzzle boxes. Tactile, creepy, perfect for a single sitting.

title: Bayonetta
blurb: Stylish action at maximum volume. Combos reward creativity. Witch time never gets old.

title: FINAL FANTASY VIII
blurb: Draw magic from enemies, junction it to stats. Weird system, underrated story.

title: Spelunky 2
blurb: Harder than the first. Every death teaches something. Bottomless depth in a platformer.

title: Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons
blurb: One controller, two brothers. Three hours. The mechanic IS the story.

title: Mass Effect 3
blurb: The trilogy closer. Combat's the best it's been. Bring tissues for Tuchanka.

title: Sid Meier's Civilization V
blurb: One more turn. You know the trap. Fall in willingly.

title: Into the Breach
blurb: Tiny mech tactics, perfect information. Every move matters. Short runs, huge depth.

title: Diablo IV
blurb: Loot, click, loot, click. The loop works. Dark fantasy comfort food.

title: Animal Crossing: New Horizons
blurb: Your island, your pace. 20 minutes a day adds up to something real.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Phantom Hourglass
blurb: DS Zelda with stylus controls. The ocean exploration works.

title: Forza Horizon 4
blurb: Open-world driving through Britain's seasons. Not a sim. A road trip with 500 cars.

title: Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy
blurb: PS2 platformer with no loading screens. Bright, breezy, a weekend clear.

title: Black & White
blurb: Play god. Literally. Train a giant creature, answer prayers, smite villages.

title: The Witness
blurb: An island of line puzzles. Sounds boring. Isn't. Teaches without a single word.

title: Inside
blurb: Three hours of pure dread. No dialogue. The ending won't leave you alone.

title: Limbo
blurb: Black and white puzzle platformer. A dead boy. A spider. You'll remember it.

title: What Remains of Edith Finch
blurb: Walk through a family house. Each room is a different kind of gutpunch.

title: Firewatch
blurb: Summer job as a fire lookout. Talk to your boss on a walkie-talkie. Feel things.

title: Gone Home
blurb: Walk through your family's empty house. Read everything. Trust the payoff.

title: Control
blurb: Federal building full of things that don't obey physics. The Ashtray Maze alone.

title: Psychonauts 2
blurb: Platforming through people's brains. Funnier and more thoughtful than it has any right to be.

title: Titanfall 2
blurb: Wall-running mech shooter with a 6-hour campaign that punches above everything. Effect and Cause.

title: Doom (2016)
blurb: Rip and tear. The reboot that remembered shooters should feel good.

title: Doom Eternal
blurb: Doom 2016 cranked to eleven. Resource management disguised as ultraviolence.

title: Death Stranding
blurb: Walking simulator where 'walking simulator' is the whole point. Strangely meditative.

title: It Takes Two
blurb: Co-op mandatory. Every level reinvents the rules. Best couples therapy in gaming.

title: Cyberpunk 2077
blurb: Night City earned its reputation. The side stories are the real main quest.

title: Horizon Zero Dawn
blurb: Robot dinosaurs in a post-apocalypse. The lore reveal is worth the 25 hours alone.

title: Ghost of Tsushima
blurb: Samurai open world. Wind guides you, not waypoints. Kurosawa mode exists for a reason.

title: Spiritfarer
blurb: Ferry the dead to the afterlife. Hug them first. Will absolutely make you cry.

title: Cocoon
blurb: Worlds inside worlds inside worlds. Puzzle design that makes you feel like a genius.

title: The Talos Principle
blurb: First-person puzzles with philosophical arguments. Patient, smart, surprisingly moving.

title: Prey (2017)
blurb: Anything in the room could be a mimic. Immersive sim in a space station. Criminally overlooked.

title: Dead Cells
blurb: Metroidvania roguelite. Every run feels tighter. The combat clicks and never lets go.

title: Dark Souls
blurb: The one that started it. Lordran rewards curiosity and punishes greed.

title: Dark Souls III
blurb: Fastest Souls game. The boss fights are the high point. Finale the series earned.

title: Returnal
blurb: Die, loop, improve. Roguelite bullet hell with a story that earns the confusion.

title: Alan Wake 2
blurb: Survival horror where the writer is the weapon. The musical number is real.

title: Resident Evil Village
blurb: Tall vampire lady. Also: tight horror with wild pacing. Each section a different flavor.

title: Resident Evil 4 (2023)
blurb: The remake that nailed it. Tense, replayable, the attache case still satisfies.

title: Hi-Fi Rush
blurb: Rhythm action brawler. Everything moves to the beat. Pure joy, criminally undersold.

title: Sifu
blurb: You age every time you die. Kung fu roguelite. Tight, brutal, fair.

title: Neon White
blurb: FPS speedrunning with cards. Chase the leaderboard. One more try. One more.

title: Katana ZERO
blurb: One-hit-kill action platformer. Time slows, you plan, you execute.

title: Oxenfree
blurb: Teens on an island with a radio. The dialogue system feels genuinely natural.

title: Night in the Woods
blurb: College dropout returns to a dying town. Platforming and feelings in equal measure.

title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice
blurb: Psychosis rendered as game mechanics. Headphones mandatory. Short, searing, important.

title: Ori and the Blind Forest
blurb: Gorgeous Metroidvania that will make you cry in the first ten minutes.

title: Cuphead
blurb: 1930s cartoon boss rush. Frame-perfect art, frame-perfect difficulty. Worth the pain.

title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
blurb: Midgar expanded into a full game. The combat system earned the reimagining.

title: Yakuza 0
blurb: Crime drama that pivots to karaoke, real estate, and bowling. Absurd and sincere.

title: 13 Sentinels: Aegis Rim
blurb: 13 timelines, mechs, time travel. Vanillaware made a visual novel that actually needs a flowchart.

title: Little Nightmares
blurb: Tiny child, huge monsters. Three hours of perfectly paced dread.

title: A Plague Tale: Innocence
blurb: Siblings surviving the Black Death. The rat swarms are unforgettable.

title: Transistor
blurb: Cyberpunk action RPG with a talking sword. The combat pauses for strategy. Gorgeous.

title: Bastion
blurb: The narrator reacts to everything you do. That hook never gets old.

title: Pyre
blurb: Magical basketball with exiles. Losing changes the story. Supergiant's most underrated.

title: The Forgotten City
blurb: Roman time loop mystery. Started as a Skyrim mod. Better than it had any right to be.

title: Chicory: A Colorful Tale
blurb: You paint the world. Literally. Gentle adventure about creativity and self-doubt.

title: Stray
blurb: You are a cat. In a robot city. The meow button exists. Enough said.

title: Her Story
blurb: Search a police database. Watch interview clips. Piece it together yourself.

title: Citizen Sleeper
blurb: Tabletop RPG on a space station. Dice determine your day. Gorgeous writing.

title: Loop Hero
blurb: Build the road. Watch the hero walk it. Tile placement roguelite. Deceptively deep.

title: Hyper Light Drifter
blurb: Pixel art action with zero dialogue. The world tells its own story through color and ruin.

title: CrossCode
blurb: Action RPG disguised as an MMO. The puzzle dungeons rival Zelda. Criminally long and worth it.

title: Astro Bot
blurb: Pure 3D platforming joy. Every level a toy box. PlayStation's answer to Mario.

title: Animal Well
blurb: Looks simple. Has layers. Puzzle Metroidvania that keeps revealing secrets weeks later.

title: Outer Worlds
blurb: Fallout in space, smaller scope, sharper writing. Doesn't overstay its welcome.

title: Monster Hunter: World
blurb: Hunt monsters, wear them, hunt bigger monsters. The loop is perfect. Bring friends or don't.

title: Celeste 2: Lani's Trek
blurb: Tiny follow-up to Celeste. Free, short, still surprisingly hard.

title: The Pathless
blurb: Run, glide, hunt. Open world with no map. The movement is the reward.

title: Unpacking
blurb: Unpack boxes into apartments. A life told through stuff. Quiet and devastating.

title: Hades II
blurb: Supergiant did it again. Melinoe fights differently, the story cuts just as deep.

title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
blurb: French RPG with real consequences. The art direction alone justifies the hours.

title: Final Fantasy XVI
blurb: Eikon fights shake the screen. More action than RPG. Clive's story earns every hour.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man 2
blurb: Two Spider-Men, one New York. Symbiote arc hits. The swinging still feels perfect.

title: Dave the Diver
blurb: Dive by day, run a sushi restaurant by night. Shouldn't work. Absolutely works.

title: Dredge
blurb: Fishing game with Lovecraftian dread. The catch of the day might have too many eyes.

title: Lies of P
blurb: Pinocchio souls-like. Sounds absurd. Plays beautifully. The weapon system is genuinely clever.

title: Super Mario Bros. Wonder
blurb: Mario got weird again. Talking flowers, elephant Mario, every level a surprise.

title: Pikmin 4
blurb: Grow tiny plant soldiers, solve puzzles, fight bugs. Charming, strategic, weirdly relaxing.

title: Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon
blurb: FromSoft made a mech game and it plays like a boss rush. Build, fight, rebuild.

title: Black Myth: Wukong
blurb: Monkey King action RPG. Visually staggering, mechanically tight. Boss fights demand respect.

title: Metaphor: ReFantazio
blurb: Atlus made a fantasy Persona. 80 hours of systems, story, and style. Worth every minute.

title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
blurb: Midgar to the Forgotten Capital. Open world that earns the detours. The Gold Saucer alone.

title: Silent Hill 2 (2024)
blurb: The remake that understood the assignment. Fog, guilt, and creature design that disturbs.

title: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth
blurb: Ichiban goes to Hawaii. Turn-based RPG with minigame madness. Heart on its sleeve.

title: UFO 50
blurb: Fifty fake retro games. Most of them are genuinely great. The meta-game ties them together.

title: Lorelei and the Laser Eyes
blurb: Black and white puzzle game. Every room a riddle. Aesthetic commitment that rewards patience.

title: Neva
blurb: Wordless companion adventure. Short, gorgeous, emotionally devastating.

title: Senua's Saga: Hellblade II
blurb: Iceland as a nightmare. Seven hours of sensory overload. Headphones still mandatory.

title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle
blurb: MachineGames made an Indy game and it works. Vatican, pyramids, punching Nazis.

title: Thank Goodness You're Here!
blurb: Slapstick adventure in a Yorkshire town. Two hours of genuine belly laughs.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man: Miles Morales
blurb: Shorter, tighter, Miles's own story. The venom powers feel electric.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered
blurb: Web-slinging through Manhattan never stopped feeling good. The benchmark superhero game.

title: Remnant II
blurb: Souls-like shooter with procedural worlds. Co-op shines. Solo works. Every run different.

title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2
blurb: Chainsword through a Tyranid horde. Eight hours of spectacle, zero pretense.

title: Townscaper
blurb: Click to build a town. No goals, no failure. Just colors and rooftops.

title: PowerWash Simulator
blurb: Pressure-wash dirt off things. Aggressively satisfying. The podcast game.

title: Katamari Damacy REROLL
blurb: Roll things into a ball. Bigger and bigger. The soundtrack is permanent.

title: Untitled Goose Game
blurb: Be a horrible goose. Ruin a village. Honk button. That's the whole game.

title: Dorfromantik
blurb: Tile-placement puzzle. Build countryside landscapes. Meditative and endlessly replayable.

title: Abzu
blurb: Swim with whales. No combat, no death. Journey, but underwater.

title: Gris
blurb: Watercolor platformer about grief. No words needed. Every frame a painting.

title: Kind Words
blurb: Write letters to strangers. Get letters back. Radical sincerity in lo-fi form.

title: Eastshade
blurb: Open-world game where you paint landscapes. No combat. Pure exploration and vibes.

title: Wylde Flowers
blurb: Farm sim with actual witchcraft. Cozy surface, surprisingly good story underneath.

title: The Gardens Between
blurb: Manipulate time on tiny islands. Friendship told through objects. Short and warm.

title: Soma
blurb: Underwater horror that asks what makes you you. The safe mode is legit.

title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
blurb: Can't fight back. Can only hide. The game that launched a thousand streamers.

title: Phasmophobia
blurb: Ghost hunting with friends and a spirit box. Terrifying until someone screams, then hilarious.

title: Peglin
blurb: Peggle meets roguelite. Bounce orbs, deal damage, zone out completely.

title: Webbed
blurb: You're a tiny spider building webs. Swinging physics, no stakes, pure charm.

title: Wandersong
blurb: Sing your way through problems. Relentlessly positive. Will sneak up and move you.

title: Teardown
blurb: Voxel destruction sandbox. Plan a heist, then smash walls to execute it. Endlessly satisfying.

title: Overcooked 2
blurb: Co-op cooking chaos. Friendships tested. Kitchens on fire. Someone chop the onions.

title: Trombone Champ
blurb: Rhythm game where you're bad at trombone on purpose. Genuine art form.

title: Celeste 64: Fragments of the Mountain
blurb: Fifteen minutes of 3D Celeste. Free. The dash still feels perfect.

title: Before Your Eyes
blurb: Blink to advance time. Your webcam controls the game. Two hours, permanent damage.

title: To the Moon
blurb: Walk through someone's memories backward. RPG Maker, no combat, devastating.

title: Florence
blurb: A relationship told in vignettes. Thirty minutes. Touchscreen interactions that mean something.

title: Venba
blurb: Cook Tamil recipes. Raise a son. Immigration story told through food. Ninety minutes, stays.

title: Baba Is You
blurb: Push the words that make the rules. The puzzles will break your brain. Brilliantly.

title: Papers, Please
blurb: Stamp passports. Follow rules. Feel the moral weight. Glory to Arstotzka.

title: Sable
blurb: Hoverbike across a desert. Find your mask. Open world with nothing to kill.

title: Viewfinder
blurb: Take a photo, place it in the world, walk into it. Perspective puzzles done fresh.

title: Resident Evil 2 (2019)
blurb: The remake that set the standard. Mr. X is always coming. Two campaigns, both essential.

title: Pathologic 2
blurb: Plague town survival. Deliberately hostile. The greatest game you might not enjoy playing.

title: Darkest Dungeon
blurb: Your heroes will go mad. Manage their sanity and yours. Narrator alone is worth it.

title: Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree
blurb: DLC-sized like a full game. The Lands of Shadow out-creep the base game.

title: Factorio
blurb: Automate everything. Conveyor belts stretch to the horizon. You will dream about production ratios.

title: Satisfactory
blurb: 3D factory building in a gorgeous world. Optimize, rebuild, optimize again. The factory must grow.

title: Minecraft
blurb: Build anything. Mine everything. You already know. You just haven't gone back lately.

title: Terraria
blurb: 2D Minecraft with actual bosses. Dig down, gear up, fight gods. Endlessly generous.

title: No Man's Sky
blurb: Explore a universe. Or just build a base. Redemption arc of the decade.

title: Rimworld
blurb: Colony sim that generates stories. Your pawns will do terrible things. You'll let them.

title: Cities: Skylines
blurb: Build a city. Watch traffic break it. Fix the traffic. That is the game.

title: Anno 1800
blurb: Industrial revolution city builder. Supply chains, trade routes, islands. Time vanishes.

title: Planet Zoo
blurb: Design a zoo. Obsess over enclosures. The animals judge your paths. You fix the paths.

title: Astroneer
blurb: Terraforming sandbox on alien planets. Chill, colorful, surprisingly deep.

title: Borderlands 3
blurb: A billion procedural guns. Shoot the thing, pick up the loot. Pure dopamine loop.

title: Just Cause 3
blurb: Grapple hook, wingsuit, explosions. The open world is your demolition playground.

title: The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth
blurb: Crying baby fights nightmares. Thousands of item combos. Every run different. Bottomless.

title: LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga
blurb: Nine movies, one game. Brick everything. Great with a kid or without one.

title: Risk of Rain 2
blurb: Third-person roguelite that scales until the screen melts. Loop until you can't.

title: Far Cry 6
blurb: Tropical dictatorship sandbox. The formula works when you stop thinking about it.

title: Grim Dawn
blurb: Diablo-style ARPG with build depth. Dark, crunchy, doesn't demand your full attention.

title: Monster Train
blurb: Slay the Spire on a train going to hell. Faster, wilder, equally addictive.

title: Brotato
blurb: Arena survivor with a potato. Fast runs, absurd builds. Pure one-more-run energy.

title: Assassin's Creed Odyssey
blurb: Ancient Greece is gorgeous. Climb, fight, sail. Turn your brain off for 40 hours.

title: Life is Strange
blurb: Time-rewind teen drama. Choices haunt. The soundtrack will follow you home.

title: The Last Guardian
blurb: A boy and his giant bird-dog. Frustrating, beautiful, unforgettable.

title: Omori
blurb: RPG that hides something dark. Cute surface, devastating underneath. Don't spoil it.

title: STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl
blurb: The Zone doesn't care about you. Ukrainian post-apocalypse with atmosphere for days.

title: The Forest
blurb: Crash-land. Build shelter. Discover what's in the caves. Survival horror that earns both words.

title: Alien: Isolation
blurb: One xenomorph. No weapons that matter. Fifteen hours of hiding under desks.

title: Project Zomboid
blurb: Zombie survival sim. You will die. The question is how long you can delay it.

title: Dead Space (2023)
blurb: The remake that made the Ishimura gorgeous and terrible. Plasma cutter still king.

title: Metro Exodus
blurb: Post-apocalyptic train journey through Russia. Linear shooter that opens up. Gorgeous bleakness.

title: The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker
blurb: Cel-shaded Zelda that aged better than every 'realistic' game around it.
